items.title: ATLAS: Agentic or Latent Visual Reasoning? One Word is Enough for Both
items.authors: Ziyu Guo, Rain Liu, Xinyan Chen et al.
items.abstract: Visual reasoning, often interleaved with intermediate visual states, has emerged as a promising direction in the field. A straightforward approach is to directly generate images via unified models during reasoning, but this is computationally expensive and architecturally non-trivial. Recent alterna...

items.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
items.authors: Shashwat Goel, Nikhil Chandak, Arvindh Arun et al.
items.abstract: AI agents are being increasingly deployed in dynamic, open-ended environments that require adapting to new information as it arrives. To efficiently measure this capability for realistic use-cases, we propose building grounded simulations that replay real-world events in the order they occurred. We ...

items.title: RefDecoder: Enhancing Visual Generation with Conditional Video Decoding
items.authors: Xiang Fan, Yuheng Wang, Bohan Fang et al.
items.abstract: Video generation powers a vast array of downstream applications. However, while the de facto standard, i.e., latent diffusion models, typically employ heavily conditioned denoising networks, their decoders often remain unconditional. We observe that this architectural asymmetry leads to significant ...
